sentence_reviews.text: Continuous waveforms and time–frequency representations encode speech, music, acoustics, and sensor streams.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: sequence models and 1d CNNs for sequence classification/generation (lines 463-478); AIMA contents.html: speech recognition and speech synthesis in natural language tasks / communicating, perceiving, and acting (lines 621-642).
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected sources support speech and sequence modeling, but they do not directly mention music, acoustics, sensor streams, or time-frequency representations.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Waveforms, audio & spectrograms is to connect Signal processing & harmonic analysis, Sequences, events & time series to downstream capabilities including Multimodal, simulated & streaming data, Speech recognition & spoken-language understanding, Speech synthesis, voice & music generation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: sequence models and 1d CNNs (lines 463-478); AIMA contents.html: NLP language/speech-related chapter context, natural language tasks, and communicating/perceiving/acting part headings (lines 621-642).
sentence_reviews.notes: The evidence supports sequence modeling and speech/NLP application context, but signal processing, harmonic analysis, spectrograms, music generation, and multimodal/simulated/streaming data are not directly supported.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Multimodal, simulated & streaming data
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: data section with image and text datasets and preprocessing (lines 15-20), plus sequence models (lines 463-478).
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected evidence does not directly support audio signals as a substrate for multimodal, simulated, or streaming data.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Speech recognition & spoken-language understanding
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html: Part VI 'Communicating, perceiving, and acting', Chapter 23 natural language processing and natural language tasks (lines 621-642).
sentence_reviews.notes: The AIMA TOC supports language tasks and communication/perception context, but the inspected headings do not explicitly say audio waveforms or spectrograms are the substrate for speech recognition.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Speech synthesis, voice & music generation
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: sequence generation with 1d CNNs, RNNs, LSTMs, and transformers (lines 463-504); AIMA contents.html: natural language tasks and communication/perception context (lines 621-642).
sentence_reviews.notes: Sequence generation is supported, and speech/language context is present. Voice and music generation are not directly supported by the inspected passages.

sentence_reviews.text: Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Signal processing & harmonic analysis, Sequences, events & time series.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: sequence models and preprocessing data (lines 18-20 and 463-504); AIMA contents.html: fairness and bias plus sequence/probabilistic reasoning over time (lines 745-749 and 361-382).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sequence dependency and broad bias concern are supported. Harmonic analysis, information loss from audio encodings, and invariance difficulty are not directly supported.

sentence_reviews.text: Waveforms, audio & spectrograms rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Signal processing & harmonic analysis.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: 1d CNNs for sequence classification/generation and sequence models (lines 463-478).
sentence_reviews.notes: Signal-processing relevance is plausible for waveforms and spectrograms, but the inspected source does not directly cover signal processing or harmonic analysis.

sentence_reviews.text: Waveforms, audio & spectrograms consumes, emits, or organizes information using Sequences, events & time series.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: neural networks for sequences and sequence generation/classification (lines 463-478); AIMA contents.html: probabilistic reasoning over time and NLP sequence/language models (lines 361-382 and 621-630).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support sequence/time modeling; applying that to waveforms and spectrograms is synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: Waveforms, audio & spectrograms consumes, emits, or organizes information using Scalars, vectors, matrices & tensors.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: linear algebra data matrices and sequence models (lines 206-259 and 463-504); AIMA contents.html: tensor operations in deep learning and sequence/language models (lines 561-575 and 621-660).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support tensor/vector/matrix representations and sequence models, but not audio tensorization in exact wording.

edge_reviews.notes: The dependency is plausible, but the cited packet references do not directly evidence signal processing or harmonic analysis for audio.
Audio waveforms are naturally sequence/time data, and sequence modeling is directly supported. The represents_with/core edge is defensible.
Vector/matrix/tensor representations are broadly supported for ML sequence models. Common strength is defensible because tensors are representational rather than the audio-domain concept itself.
